results.title: Addressing Reasons for Underutilisation of Parental Leave Benefits
results.summary: The Family Trends Report 2025 indicates high take-up rates for Government-Paid Maternity Leave (74–79%) and Government-Paid Paternity Leave (47–56%) between 2016 and 2023, with Shared Parental Leave data pending as it was implemented in April. Underutilisation stems from workplace norms, job nature, and societal gender perceptions. To address this, the Government made additional paternity leave and Shared Parental Leave mandatory, extended dismissal protections, and introduced a four-week notice period for paternity and adoption leave to encourage early planning. The Workplace Fairness Act will further protect against discrimination based on pregnancy or caregiving. The Government is pursuing a whole-of-society approach, urging businesses to adopt supportive HR practices and collaborate with employees to balance productivity with parental support.

results.title: Addressing and Regulating Concerns of Dog Urine in Public Areas
results.summary: The Ministry for Sustainability and the Environment stated there are no plans to mandate cleaning up pet urine, as complete removal is difficult compared to defecation. Instead, the approach focuses on fostering responsible pet ownership and civic-mindedness to prevent bad smells and stains. Pet owners are urged to wash urine off using water from bottles or other means to uphold public hygiene. The National Environment Agency and National Parks Board collaborate with Town Councils to educate owners via posters and materials. The public can provide feedback to Town Councils or relevant agencies regarding disamenities caused by pet urination for necessary action.

results.title: ActiveSG Credit Top-ups to Encourage Sustained Participation in Sports and Exercise
results.summary: The Ministry will study the technical feasibility of converting Health Promotion Board (HPB) Healthpoints into ActiveSG credits on MyActiveSG+ and will provide updates when ready. ActiveSG credits are extended automatically: members using credits once annually receive an extension to the following year, while all unused credits during the SG60 year are extended to 31 December 2026. Recent top-ups include $100 for children aged four to 12 in 2023 and a $100 SG60 top-up for all ages this year. Initial $100 credits were provided to all new members since 2014 to encourage active lifestyles, contributing to a visitorship increase of more than 60% at facilities.

results.title: Adjournment of Debate
results.summary: Mr Sharael Taha moved to adjourn the debate on the Adjournment of Debate. The Speaker granted consent for the debate to conclude immediately. The session will resume tomorrow. No further policy measures, timelines, or operational details were discussed during this brief procedural exchange.

results.title: Adjusted Attrition Rates for Teachers Leaving Full-time Service in Past Three Years
results.summary: The Ministry for Education's annual teacher resignation rate includes all who left full-time service, even those joining the Flexi-Adjunct Teaching (FAJT) Scheme. The average rate from 2022 to 2024 was 2.4%. Excluding FAJT participants, the adjusted attrition rate for the same period averaged 1.7%. As of end-2024, the Ministry provides these adjusted figures reflecting only those who exited the teaching profession entirely.

results.title: Age-appropriate SkillsFuture Scheme for Primary to Post-secondary Students from Lower-income Households
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) states that subsidising access to a wider marketplace via a SkillsFuture scheme is less effective than providing structured, school-curated opportunities and direct financial support to lower-income students. Current measures include the Junior Sports Academy Programme, Opportunity Fund grants for co-curricular development, and Student Care Centres where lower-income families pay as little as $5 per month after subsidies. For interests not covered by schools, MOE encourages approaching teachers to form interest groups or engaging community partners through grassroots leaders. Regarding the suggestion to expand Edusave ringfencing for out-of-school learning costs, the Minister confirmed that the Ministry will continue to examine how to open up access to funds for students to seize opportunities both inside and outside the classroom.

results.title: Allowing Greater Flexibility for CDC Voucher Redemption for Residents with Limited Chain Supermarket Access
results.summary: The Minister clarified that while the CDC Voucher Scheme was expanded in 2023 to allow half of the vouchers to be used at supermarkets, the remaining half must be spent at heartland merchants to support local businesses. For residents in estates with limited supermarket access, the Ministry has onboarded small businesses on a case-by-case basis rather than granting blanket flexibility for all local merchants. To date, over 23,000 hawkers and heartland merchants have been onboarded, with CDCs continuing to collaborate with partners like FMAS and HECS to identify eligible merchants in HDB heartlands. The current balanced approach aims to meet resident needs while sustaining heartland commerce, though no specific timeline for further scheme adjustments was provided.

results.title: Annual Food Poisoning Cases Resulting from Home-based Food Businesses
results.summary: Over the past five years, the Singapore Food Agency (SFA) investigated an average of one annual suspected food poisoning case linked to home-based food businesses. Investigations consistently found no causative link between the food sold and the reported incidents. No specific actions were taken in these cases as no causal connection was established. The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment provided these statistics regarding the annual frequency and investigation outcomes for such incidents.

results.title: Appeals for Waiver of 15-month Wait-out Period for Private Property Homeowners Over Past Three Years
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the specific data regarding the number of appeals to waive the 15-month wait-out period for former private homeowners, broken down by year and flat size (5-room or larger), was not provided in this session. Instead, he directed the inquiry to Parliamentary Question 112 on the Order Paper dated 22 September 2025, which contains the requested statistics. No new policy measures, eligibility changes, or operational constraints were announced during this exchange.

results.title: Applicants on Waiting List for Public HV Parking Lots and Availability of Public and Private HV Parking Lots over Past Three Years
results.summary: As of September 2025, approximately 40,000 public and private heavy vehicle (HV) parking lots exist islandwide, serving around 31,000 HVs registered under the Land Transport Authority's Vehicle Parking Certificate (VPC) scheme over the past three years. The VPC scheme mandates an overnight parking lot for registration or road tax renewal, though applicants with existing lots may wait for a preferred location. Waiting times for public HV lots vary by region based on individual park turnover rates, current waitlist lengths, and available lot counts. The Minister noted that agencies will continue monitoring demand to ensure sufficient islandwide capacity. Specific waiting list numbers and median durations for the North, South, East, West, and Central regions were not provided in the response.

results.title: Assessing Effectiveness of Adult Learning Programmes in Growth Sectors
results.summary: The Ministry of Education and SkillsFuture Singapore assess adult learning programme effectiveness using employment metrics, wage premiums, and the Training Quality and Outcomes Measure (TRAQOM) survey, with results published on the MySkillsFuture portal. As of December 2024, approximately 55% of unemployed trainees in the SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme secured jobs within six months, while Work-Study graduates reported wage premiums of 6% to 11%. A granular sectoral breakdown is currently not feasible due to sample size limitations, though some programmes equip learners with horizontal skills applicable across multiple sectors. The Ministry will continue reviewing reporting methods to better guide learners, employers, and training providers, acknowledging that wage effects do not fully capture outcomes like job retention or skill acquisition for future roles.

results.title: Assessing Risks of Sinkhole Formation
results.summary: The Building and Construction Authority (BCA) enforces a rigorous regulatory regime requiring project owners to monitor ground movements during large-scale underground works. Under the Building Control Act, BCA investigates contraventions and penalizes errant parties. Regarding the July 2025 sinkhole incident, investigations are currently ongoing. BCA will review its existing measures and determine if updates are necessary only after these investigations conclude. No specific new measures or revised timelines have been announced at this stage.

results.title: Assessment of Johor Bahru-Singapore RTS on Traffic Congestion at Land Checkpoints and Measures to Maximise Commuter Adoption
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that the Johor Bahru-Singapore RTS Link has a maximum capacity of 10,000 passengers per hour in each direction. While earlier studies suggest it could considerably alleviate Causeway traffic, this assessment depends on assumptions to be reviewed closer to project completion. The system offers a five-minute trip requiring single immigration clearance at departure and connects directly to Woodlands North MRT on the Thomson-East Coast Line. The Ministry will review specific measures to encourage commuter adoption and maximise the system's effectiveness in easing congestion.

results.title: Assessment on Fair Tenancy Code of Conduct’s Effectiveness towards Lease Renewal Rental Hikes for SMEs
results.summary: The Ministry assesses the Fair Tenancy Code of Conduct as a facilitator for balanced negotiations rather than a direct prevention of rental hikes, noting a 19% rise in retail and F&B establishments from 2018 to 2023 indicates strong demand. The Government has no plans to legislate CPI-linked rental caps for lease renewals absent material alterations or capital improvements, citing potential market distortion and reduced rental supply. Consequently, rental outcomes remain private agreements between landlords and tenants reflecting current supply and demand conditions without statutory intervention on increase limits.

results.title: Average Wait Time for Placement in Special Education School from Application to Decision
results.summary: For Primary 1 enrolment in 2025, the average processing time from application to place confirmation at a Special Education (SPED) school is six months, though vacancy constraints can extend this duration. Between 2022 and 2024, total SPED school places rose from 7,300 to 8,300 following the opening of seven new schools. The Ministry of Education plans to increase capacity further, targeting approximately 10,000 places by 2030 through existing school expansions and one additional new school. To support this growth, MOE collaborates with Social Service Agencies to attract and retain staff via enhanced salary guidelines under the SPED Teaching Journeys of Excellence Package 2.0, announced in 2024, alongside strengthened professional development opportunities.

results.title: Average and Median Salaries Earned by Public Sector Healthcare Workers from 2021 to 2025
results.summary: Between 2021 and 2024, median monthly base salaries for public sector doctors rose from approximately $13,700 to $14,400, while nurses' salaries increased from $4,300 to $4,700; these figures exclude bonuses and allowances. The Ministry of Health clarified that the previous distinction between "specialist" and "non-specialist" doctors is no longer used under the new care team model, which now defines three career tracks: family physicians, hospital clinicians, and specialists. Data for 2020 was not collected due to the COVID-19 pandemic, and the reported figures reflect the seniority profile of the workforce.

results.title: Balancing Pace of Expanding MRT Network and Upgrading Current Lines
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that the question regarding plans to upgrade the North East Line (NEL) and the Ministry's approach to balancing MRT network expansion with line upgrades was addressed in a written reply. This response was issued for Question Nos 30 and 31 for the oral answer on 23 September Parliament Sitting. No further details on specific upgrade timelines, funding, or operational constraints were provided in this transcript.

results.title: Cancellation of RSAF F-15 Base in Guam and Strategic Implications for Long-term Training
results.summary: The Ministry of Defence and US Department of War mutually agreed to discontinue discussions for an RSAF F-15 fighter training detachment in Guam after reviewing overseas training requirements, with incurred costs limited to design works as no infrastructure was constructed. Despite this cancellation, Singapore-US defence relations remain excellent and longstanding, reaffirmed by recent high-level meetings. The RSAF will continue periodic short-term training and exercises in Guam, including an upcoming detachment in October to November, while the US supports existing detachments and a new F-35 training facility is being established in Arkansas.

results.title: Collecting Employment Data of Road Users Involved in Traffic Accidents to Facilitate Targeted Interventions
results.summary: The Traffic Police do not track the occupation or employer identity of road users involved in accidents, as this data is not material to investigations. Instead, the Ministry of Manpower tracks injuries to employees and platform workers from work-related traffic accidents based on reports from employers and operators. The Ministry notifies MOM of specific incidents, such as fatalities. Consequently, interventions and public communications are targeted at vehicle owners and users based on vehicle weight classes, which correlate with injury and fatality risk, rather than individual employment details.

results.title: Compilation of Biannual Sales Data for Gross Turnover Rent and Ensuring Fair Negotiation Standards
results.summary: The Code of Conduct requires landlords collecting gross-turnover rent to share aggregated sales data by trade category with tenants before lease signing and biannually thereafter. Currently, no industry-wide standard exists for trade categorisation, and evolving business models may impact classification. The Fair Tenancy Industry Committee is studying these issues to potentially mandate like-for-like standards and prevent aggregation across non-equivalent categories. This review is expected to be completed in the first half of 2026.

results.title: Completion and Outcomes of Referrals for National Mindline 1771 Service Callers and Follow-up Care
results.summary: The Ministry tracks referral completion for identified callers to the National Mindline 1771 service, noting that most providers acknowledge referrals within three working days. Personnel follow up with providers if acknowledgments are delayed, while respective providers subsequently track client appointments, treatment compliance, and clinical outcomes. For anonymous callers requesting follow-on services, personnel determine appropriate options like General Practitioner services and provide relevant access information. The transcript does not specify data collection metrics for anonymous referrals or detailed timelines for provider-initiated follow-ups beyond the initial acknowledgment period.

results.title: Consideration of Halal Food Establishments for Participation in CDC Vouchers Scheme
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth confirmed that since October 2021, Community Development Councils have actively engaged both halal and non-halal hawkers and merchants for the CDC Vouchers Scheme. To date, over 23,000 hawkers and heartland merchants have been onboarded through collaborations with associations like the Federation of Merchants' Association and Heartland Enterprise Centre Singapore. The Ministry continues to reach out to a broad spectrum of merchants to ensure Singaporean households have diverse spending options, thereby avoiding the disadvantage of Muslim households. No specific eligibility criteria or future timeline for expanding halal participation were stated.

results.title: Considerations of Complete Road Resurfacing Versus Spot Resurfacing
results.summary: LTA decides between complete or spot road resurfacing based on work circumstances. Spot resurfacing occurs when multiple parties work concurrently on portions of a road or when works are staged; once completed, the road is permanently reinstated. Conversely, complete resurfacing is part of LTA's planned maintenance programme covering entire stretches. All resurfacing, whether partial or full, must strictly adhere to LTA's requirements and undergo audit checks to ensure compliance.

results.title: Consolidating and Reviewing Scope of Wildlife Management Strategies and Responsibilities
results.summary: The Minister for National Development explained that NParks employs science and community-based strategies tailored to species-specific risks, ecology, and behavior, utilizing measures like population control and food source reduction. While Town Councils (TCs) possess local environmental knowledge and may approach NParks for advice on localized issues, the Ministry will not consolidate wildlife management responsibilities under a single agency or expand TC oversight to broader wildlife matters. NParks will continue supporting TCs for efficient management, maintaining the current framework where TCs handle specific local issues with NParks assistance rather than direct authority over general wildlife strategies.

results.title: Career Impact of SkillsFuture Credits, Mid-Career Training Allowance and Level-Up Programme
results.summary: The Ministry does not track specific employment status, hours worked, or wage outcomes for SkillsFuture Level-Up participants, though it aims to ensure reskilling opportunities exist at any career stage rather than mandating immediate credit utilisation. While the one-off $500 SkillsFuture Credit top-up has a fixed expiry date with approximately 70% of eligible households reporting non-use, the government clarifies that there is no rush for most credits, allowing individuals to sample courses to build lifelong learning habits. Eligibility for the Mid-Career Training Allowance remains restricted to citizens aged 40 and above, with no immediate plans to extend this to workers in their 30s despite discussions on supporting postgraduate studies. Future expansion of postgraduate support under SkillsFuture is contingent upon resolving supply constraints in critical and general sectors, with 2025 usage statistics for credits and allowances to be compared against 2024 figures.

results.title: Coordination between SPF and ICA for Individuals whose Passports Have Been Impounded due to Ongoing Investigations
results.summary: Established coordination protocols exist between the Singapore Police Force (SPF) and Immigration and Checkpoints Authority (ICA) for individuals whose passports are impounded due to ongoing investigations. These protocols specifically cover scenarios involving applications for new passport issuance. The Minister confirmed that ICA verifies claims of lost passports with the SPF to prevent fraudulent re-issuance, particularly when the original passport remains under police custody. No specific timeline or additional operational constraints were stated in the response.

results.title: Data on Employee Burnout by Sector
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) does not systematically collect sector-specific burnout data, though anonymised iWorkHealth results from 2024 indicate one in three employees experienced work-related stress or burnout. To address well-being, MOM and the Workplace Safety and Health Council have launched the free iWorkHealth tool, the Well-being Champions Network, and the Tripartite Advisory on Mental Health. Additional support includes the Career Health SG initiative for jobseekers, the upcoming Workbook on Workplace Mental Well-being with conversation templates, and the Total Workplace Safety and Health Programme offering free mental well-being workshops. Participation in iWorkHealth remains voluntary, meaning the sample may not fully reflect the overall workforce or specific industry levels.

results.title: Data on Individuals Who Have Left Workforce Due To Caregiving Responsibilities and Long-term Financial Impact of Caregiving-related Unemployment
results.summary: In 2024, approximately 87,100 residents were outside the labour force due to caregiving, with 85.7% being women and 20.9% under age 40; data on first-time caregivers is unavailable. The Ministry acknowledges the long-term financial impact of caregiving-related unemployment and offers interventions including SkillsFuture and Workforce Singapore reskilling programmes, the Career Conversion Programme, and the Mid-Career Pathways Programme to aid workforce re-entry. Eligible lower-wage workers and seniors may access the Workfare Income Supplement, while the Silver Support Scheme, Matched Retirement Savings Scheme, and the Majulah Package for seniors born in 1973 or earlier provide enhanced retirement and healthcare support starting January 2025. Additionally, the Tripartite Guidelines on Flexible Work Arrangement Requests, effective December last year, facilitate mutually beneficial career and caregiving balance.

results.title: Data on Physical and Mental Health of Caregivers
results.summary: The Ministry collects data on caregiver chronic conditions, service usage, burden, and mental well-being via studies like the National Population Health Survey and WiSE Study, but does not track caregiver burnout. Findings indicate higher stress for caregivers of persons with mental health issues, dementia, or post-stroke patients, prompting interventions such as hospital training and psychosocial support from Community Outreach Teams (CREST) and Community Intervention Teams (COMIT). Approximately 24,000 caregivers received support from these teams in the past three years, alongside subsidised respite services from agencies like the Agency for Integrated Care and SG Enable. The Government addresses identified gaps through community partnerships and targeted resources to equip caregivers with necessary skills and confidence.

results.title: Definition of "Local Fish Produce" and Percentage of Locally-produced Fishes Originating from Fish Stock Imported
results.summary: The Singapore Food Agency (SFA) defines "local fish produce" as fish grown or produced from fry or fingerlings by licensed local farms. SFA does not prescribe or track minimum grow-out periods, relying instead on periodic farm checks to monitor declared production data. Consequently, the agency does not currently track the percentage of labelled "local" fish originating from imported near-market stock, nor has it specified steps to prevent importers from exploiting this lack of a minimum grow-out requirement.

results.title: Development of Assistive Technology for Visually Impaired Public Transport Users
results.summary: Between 2019 and 2024, the Land Transport Authority (LTA) partnered with SG Enable and the Singapore Association of the Visually Handicapped to trial a mobile application providing real-time audio alerts for approaching buses at select stops. The LTA is currently reviewing the trial outcomes with the intention of extending the service to more bus services and areas if successful. This initiative aims to enhance independent and inclusive access to public transportation for visually impaired passengers.

results.title: Development of Recreational Water Sports Facilities along Serangoon River and Serangoon Park Connector
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated there are currently no plans to permit recreational water sports activities along the Serangoon Reservoir. This restriction specifically applies to the areas around the Serangoon Park connector along Upper Serangoon View and along Hougang Ave 7. No alternative locations or future timelines for such facilities were mentioned in the response.

results.title: Disciplinary Actions and Improvement Measures Taken Following PUB Document Falsification Incident
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment directed members to refer to the combined oral reply delivered on 22 September 2025 regarding Public Utilities Board Audit Observations for details on disciplinary actions, investigation status, and improvement measures. This response addresses questions concerning document falsification findings by the Auditor-General's Office. Specific outcomes regarding officer discipline, root cause identification, and systemic improvements beyond officer training remain detailed in that prior parliamentary record rather than in this current exchange.

results.title: Discussing Safety, Licensing and Work Permit Rules at Singapore-Malaysia Meeting to discuss Cross-border P2P Transport
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that safety, licensing, and work permit rules for cross-border point-to-point transport were addressed at the recent meeting with Malaysian representatives. However, the specific outcomes, agreed measures, or timelines resulting from this discussion were not detailed in the parliamentary record. The Minister directed Members to refer to the written reply issued for Question No 35 during the 23 September Parliament Sitting for the full details of the response. No further operational constraints or statistics were provided in this transcript.

results.title: Effectiveness of Participation in HPB’s Mental Well-being Workshops for Parents in Supporting Children’s Mental Health
results.summary: Since launching in 2020, approximately 2,600 eligible parents have participated in HPB's Mental Well-being Workshops annually. Effectiveness is measured via pre- and post-workshop surveys, which indicate that about 85% of attendees gained new knowledge and expressed an intention to apply these skills to support their children's mental well-being. The Minister did not provide data on the percentage of eligible parents who have participated or confirm if the intention to practise translates into sustained support.

results.title: Employment of S Pass Holders in Public Sector and Measures to Prioritise Local Talent
results.summary: Approximately 550 S Pass holders are employed across Ministries and Statutory Boards, constituting roughly 0.35% of the 154,000 public officers. These roles specifically require skills currently in shortage within the local workforce. The Public Service adheres to Tripartite Guidelines on Fair Employment Practices, ensuring all local candidates are fairly considered for positions. The Ministry has determined that further disaggregated data on S Pass employment is of limited public interest given the small total number.

results.title: Enforcement Operations Taken against Those Offering Non-compliant Battery or Motor Modifications for AMDs
results.summary: From 1 January 2024 to 30 August 2025, the Land Transport Authority conducted 15 enforcement operations against retailers offering non-compliant battery or motor modifications for active mobility devices. Approximately 14 retailers were investigated, with five prosecuted; four were convicted and fined between $600 and $4,000, while one received a $600 composition sum. An additional case was escalated to court following non-payment of a composition sum. Of the remaining eight cases, investigations are complete for four, pending charges for two, and ongoing for two, with no further action taken for two cases due to insufficient evidence. The Authority will continue monitoring retailer activities and taking firm action against errant retailers.

results.title: English Language Guideline Considerations towards F&B Outlets that Primarily Use Chinese Language in Stores
results.summary: There are no guidelines or regulations mandating the use of English for food and beverage shop displays, menus, or the hiring of English-speaking staff. Businesses retain full autonomy to decide on these operational matters. While the Ministry has not imposed requirements, many operators voluntarily adopt English to serve a diverse customer base, reflecting the common medium of communication in Singapore. The issue remains unaddressed by the Ministry as no specific constraints or timelines for compliance were established during the debate.

results.title: Enhancing Digital Accessibility to Public Digital Services for Seniors and Persons with Disabilities
results.summary: The Government has enhanced Digital Service Standards to prioritize usability and accessibility, ensuring digital content is readable by assistive technologies. In July, GovTech Singapore launched the A11y Playground, offering developers technical checklists and assessment tools to support inclusive design practices alongside existing resources like Oobee. Citizen engagement through Tech Kaki communities further aids in refining services based on diverse user feedback. While advancing a "digital first" approach, the Government maintains non-digital support channels, including physical service touchpoints, hotlines, and ServiceSG Centres, to ensure citizens requiring in-person assistance remain included.

results.title: Enhancing Support for Persons with Disabilities and Improving Access to Sporting Facilities
results.summary: The Enabling Masterplan 2030 guides efforts to enhance support for persons with disabilities through new programmes including the Enabling Skills for Life Programme from the fourth quarter of 2025, the Enabled Living Programme from November 2025, and the Home Support Programme from 2026. Under the refreshed Disability Sports Master Plan 2024, Sport Singapore is retrofitting public swimming pools with wheelchair-accessible ramps, with nine completed in 2023 and one more by end-2025, while expanding inclusive ActiveSG gyms from 15 to all facilities by 2026. Initiatives also include launching the Enabling Sports Fund and requiring mainstream schools to conduct disability sports programmes by 2028 to build empathy. These measures aim to improve accessibility and foster an inclusive culture for meaningful participation in society.

results.title: Expanding Provision of Shaded Public Playgrounds and Outdoor Play Areas Given Rising Average Temperatures
results.summary: Agencies like HDB and NParks integrate thermal comfort into playground planning using sun-shade analysis, wind modelling, and heat mapping to locate shaded spaces. Play areas are sited in building shadow zones where feasible, utilizing climate-suited equipment and greenery to lower ambient temperatures. NParks has established 40 nature playgardens island-wide under the City in Nature vision, offering curated outdoor spaces for children to connect with nature. These measures align with efforts to encourage real-world physical play and support healthy child development amidst rising temperatures.

results.title: Extension of Large Family LifeSG Credits to and Providing Additional Support for Blended Families with Stepchildren
results.summary: The Minister clarified that Large Family LifeSG Credits apply to blended families where one or both spouses have remarried and care for three or more Singaporean children, including those from previous marriages. Eligibility is administratively verified via Child Development Account (CDA) trusteeship, with credits of $1,000 annually per third and subsequent child aged one to six. Beyond this scheme, all Singaporean children in blended families receive universal benefits such as education, healthcare, and childcare subsidies, alongside the Foreign Domestic Worker Levy Concession. Families needing further assistance can access financial aid through Social Service Offices or holistic support at Family Service Centres.

results.title: Factors for Continuation of Government's Cost-of-living Support
results.summary: The Government determines cost-of-living support continuation holistically, weighing inflation, labour market conditions, fiscal sustainability, and affordability challenges for lower- and middle-income households. While inflation has moderated, elevated price levels justify transitional support like CDC vouchers and U-Save rebates, alongside strengthened structural aid in housing, healthcare, education, and retirement. Support decisions rely on data-verified needs rather than unverified anxieties, with specific measures targeting seniors, lower-income groups, and social purposes like childcare. Future adjustments depend on monitoring economic conditions, broad-based real wage growth, and specific circumstances, rather than fixed timelines or automatic extensions.

results.title: Faulty Installation of ERP2.0 On-board Units since November 2023 and Actions Taken against Unsatisfactory Installations
results.summary: Since November 2023, approximately 9,000 cars have required rectification for faulty ERP 2.0 on-board unit (OBU) installations. These cases are evenly distributed across authorised workshops where trained technicians perform all installations. To maintain standards, the Land Transport Authority (LTA) regularly audits workshops, particularly those receiving negative feedback, and provides re-training along with installation manuals to technicians. No specific measures against unsatisfactory workshops were detailed beyond these audit and support actions.

results.title: Feasibility of Using Location-based SMS Notifications for Missing Persons Alerts on OneService App
results.summary: The Municipal Services Office currently broadcasts missing persons alerts for Dementia Singapore via OneService App push notifications to users in the relevant town, linking directly to in-app photographs and details. The Ministry has assessed and decided against implementing location-based SMS notifications because the OneService App already provides the necessary information, which cannot be delivered via SMS. Consequently, the feasibility of using SMS for these alerts is not being pursued, and recipients are directed to the app for full case details.

results.title: Foundational Gen AI Literacy and Skills for All Students, Teachers and Educators
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) integrates foundational generative AI literacy into the curriculum, co-curriculum, and enrichment programmes like Code for Fun to equip students and teachers with digital competencies. These measures specifically address critical thinking regarding AI biases, questioning outputs, and identifying false information known as "Gen AI hallucinations." Teachers receive guidance on ethical and pedagogical considerations to manage associated risks and limitations effectively. As the definition of AI literacy evolves, MOE will continue monitoring developments and consider how best to measure AI literacy in due course.

results.title: Frequency of Maintenance Works and Audits for HDB Rental Flats
results.summary: Pre-COVID-19, HDB conducted annual inspections for occupied rental flats to identify repair needs, a practice temporarily disrupted by pandemic measures and now being resumed more efficiently through bundled and outsourced inspections. Tenants remain responsible for maintenance per the tenancy agreement, though HDB assists with functional defects like ceiling leaks, water seepage, and spalling concrete. While tenants handle aesthetic issues such as peeling paint, HDB will address functional impediments like jammed gates. The Minister noted that full repainting requires tenants to vacate for belongings removal and will explore community partner support. Further details on audit frequencies and maintenance schedules will be shared next year.

results.title: Frequency of Recyclable Collection in Residential Estates' Blue Bins
results.summary: The National Environment Agency (NEA) adjusts recyclable collection frequencies based on ground conditions, having increased standard collection from once to three times weekly, while larger 2,200-litre side-loading bins are collected weekly in high-volume areas. NEA receives few monthly reports of overflowing bins, typically caused by unflattened cardboard or festive-season surges, prompting temporary frequency increases during these periods. Residents are advised to flatten cardboard boxes and clean recyclables before disposal to prevent overflow and maintain hygiene. The Ministry does not currently plan a universal shift to five-times-weekly collections but will continue reviewing schedules as needed.

results.title: Funding Distribution for Local Literature from FY2020 to FY2024, Assessment Metrics for SingLit Programmes and Development of Local Literary Ecosystem
results.summary: From FY2020 to FY2024, the National Arts Council directed approximately $20 million to support local literature across four official languages via grants, sector schemes, and events like the Singapore Writers Festival. Impact assessment combines ticket sales, attendance, readership, and professional outcomes, evidenced by the sale of over 30 title rights internationally between 2023 and 2024. The Council supports writers through creation grants, arts housing, capability development, networking, and transmedia adaptations to diversify income streams. Embedding local literature includes curricula integration, the One Story Project translating works into other official languages, and the Poems on the MRT initiative featuring over 100 poems. The Council will continue reviewing strategies to expand touchpoints and nurture a vibrant literary ecosystem responsive to community needs.

results.title: Gender and Age Profiles of Community Intervention Team Clients
results.summary: In FY2024, the Community Intervention Team (COMIT) supported over 16,000 clients, of whom 35% were male. Male clients were distributed across age groups as follows: 2% aged 18–19, 30% aged 20–34, 41% aged 35–60, and 27% above 60. Common presenting issues included depressive and anxiety disorders. To improve help-seeking behaviour, the Ministry established access points such as the national mindline 1771 hotline, the mindline.sg digital portal, and in-person Community Outreach Teams. These services are complemented by public education campaigns aimed at combating stigma and normalising health-seeking behaviour.

results.title: Government's Assumptions Underlying Assessment of Singapore's 2%-3% GDP Growth Potential
results.summary: The Ministry of Trade and Industry projects Singapore's real GDP will grow by an average of 2% to 3% annually over the next decade, supported by productivity gains of 1% to 2% despite a slowing workforce growth of around 1% due to demographic shifts. To potentially achieve above-trend growth of 3% to 4% in the near term, the Government is launching an Economic Strategy Review to anchor higher value-added industries and leverage technologies like artificial intelligence. These measures aim to secure global competitiveness and support real wage growth. The Minister noted that achieving faster growth over the next few years will strengthen the economy's long-term footing against global uncertainties, though specific timelines for the review's completion were not stated.

results.title: Grading Guidelines for Schools In Relation to Use of Generative AI in Assignments
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the question regarding grading guidelines for generative AI in assignments has already been addressed via a written answer on 24 September 2025. Members are directed to refer to the response to Questions Nos 41 and 42 in the 24 September Order Paper for the specific details. No new measures, timelines, or plans were announced during this parliamentary session. The transcript does not contain the actual guidelines or the rationale for their absence, as these were provided in the referenced written response.

results.title: Guidelines for Pickleball Play in HDB Public Courts Given Its Rising Popularity and Potential Noise Disamenities
results.summary: Under existing community noise guidelines, residents must observe quiet hours from 10:30 pm to 7:00 am in HDB common spaces, including game courts. Between January 2024 and August 2025, the Municipal Services Office recorded 701 noise complaints regarding pickleball play in HDB estates. Town Councils are implementing local measures such as advisories, mediation, and court usage restrictions. Regarding facility conversions, Town Councils may apply to HDB for consent to convert sports facilities into multi-purpose units with sound dampeners, subject to technical safety requirements and potential disamenities. Further questions from Ms Yeo Wan Ling and Mr Jackson Lam are scheduled for a subsequent Sitting.

results.title: Impact of Acquisition of RSAF P-8A Poseidon and F-35 Jets on Maritime Security Capabilities
results.summary: The acquisition of four Boeing P-8A Poseidon maritime patrol aircraft and 20 F-35 jets will augment Singapore's maritime situational awareness, counter sub-surface threats, and diversify the Republic of Singapore Air Force's future fighter fleet comprising F-35As, F-35Bs, and F-15SGs. The Ministry of Defence's capability acquisition approach ensures long-term supportability through OEM engineering support, local maintenance establishment, and comprehensive spare parts provisioning. Personnel training is being secured via USAF instructors for the initial F-35 pilot batch and a new training detachment at Ebbing Air National Guard Base for joint exercises with partner nations operating the same platforms. Specific training plans for P-8A operators are currently under development as part of a broader upgrade to the maritime security surveillance network.

results.title: Impact of Increased EV Adoption on Electricity Grid's Load
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that from 2030, Singapore will cease all new registrations of internal combustion engine cars and taxis. Between January and August this year, over 80% of new car and taxi registrations were cleaner-energy models, with more than half being electric. To support this transition, the Land Transport Authority will collaborate with the Energy Market Authority, SP PowerGrid, and other agencies to upgrade localised electrical infrastructure, including consumer switch-rooms and substations, ensuring sufficient power provision for increased EV adoption. The central pillar of the car-lite vision remains an accessible and affordable mass public transport network to enable the greatest number of people to reach their destinations with the least carbon emissions.

results.title: Impact of Rising Healthcare and Daily Living Costs on Retirement Adequacy
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower affirmed that rising healthcare and daily living costs impact retirement adequacy, with the Government strengthening the CPF system to ensure basic needs are met. Measures include reviewing the Basic Retirement Sum growth for future cohorts and providing lifelong monthly payouts via CPF LIFE to mitigate longevity risks. To balance immediate needs with future income, current CPF withdrawal rules maintain sufficient retirement funds, while the 2024 Majulah Package boosts adequacy for "Young Seniors" and offers MediSave bonuses to eligible seniors with lower savings. Support extends to the Workfare Income Supplement Scheme, Matched Retirement Savings Scheme, and the Silver Support Scheme, which saw a 20% increase in quarterly payments this year to match inflation. Additional cost-of-living relief is provided through the Assurance Package and GST Voucher schemes.

results.title: Inclusion of Films under Arts and Heritage Subsectors for SG Culture Pass
results.summary: The SG Culture Pass, launched on 1 September 2025, currently covers over 400 local arts and heritage programmes but does not yet include films. The Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth (MCCY) is actively discussing with the Singapore International Film Festival (SGIFF) and cinema operators to include eligible local films written, directed, or produced by Singaporeans. Discussions focus on incorporating film programmes and activities into the pass by the end of November 2025, pending confirmation of technical feasibility for cinema screenings. The Government aims to support local artists and works through this expansion.

results.title: Inclusion of Moderate-to-Severe Ezcema under Chronic Disease Management Programme
results.summary: The Ministry will assess moderate-to-severe eczema for inclusion in the Chronic Disease Management Programme (CDMP) following review by the Clinical Advisory Committee, which evaluates disease burden, intervention benefits, costs, and clinical guidelines. Currently, eczema is not on the CDMP, but eligible patients already access up to 75% subsidies at polyclinics and specialist outpatient clinics for treatments and medications. Additionally, patients aged 60 and above can utilize Flexi-MediSave for these expenses up to the prevailing withdrawal limit. The committee will consult clinical experts before finalizing the inclusion decision.

results.title: Inclusion of Postgraduate and Professional Certification Courses in CPF Education Loan Scheme
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower confirmed there are no plans to expand the CPF Education Loan Scheme beyond basic full-time tertiary education at local approved institutions. This decision maintains the scheme's focus on supporting retirement, healthcare, and housing savings, noting that postgraduate and professional certification courses are generally suited for working adults with sufficient savings. Instead, Singaporeans are directed to alternative financing options, including Government tuition fee loans for eligible postgraduate courses at Autonomous Universities and loans from financial institutions. Additionally, individuals may utilize SkillsFuture Credit, including the $4,000 Mid-Career Credit top-up for those aged 40 and above, to cover costs for eligible courses funded by SkillsFuture Singapore.

results.title: Increasing Number of Car-lite HDB Estates beyond Gazetted Areas
results.summary: Since 2019, the Land Transport Authority has gazetted 16 car-lite areas, including public housing estates like Tengah, Bayshore, and Ulu Pandan. The Government plans to continue assessing land use and transport plans alongside nearby amenities when considering new car-lite estates beyond currently gazetted areas. No specific timeline or additional eligibility criteria for expanding these areas were stated in the response.

results.title: Indicators of Success in AV Trials and Helping Public Transport Workers Transition to AV-related Roles
results.summary: The Ministry of Transport (MOT) and Land Transport Authority (LTA) will trial autonomous vehicles (AVs) in Punggol starting Q4 2025, with plans to expand to Tengah and other towns. All AVs must pass safety assessments at CETRAN, operate with on-board safety operators initially, and hold third-party liability insurance. Trials will address accessibility for underserved routes, including potential night services, while cybersecurity protocols follow Technical Reference 68. The Government will support transport workers through reskilling programmes for roles like safety operators and remote drivers, noting no short-term displacement for taxi and private-hire drivers due to limited initial supply. Public engagement will occur via PUDO mapping and sensitisation at community centres before full passenger services commence.

results.title: Information on Annual Management Cost as Percentage of Assets for Temasek Holdings
results.summary: The Minister referred Members to Hansard records for prior data on Temasek Holdings' annual management costs as a percentage of assets. Temasek Holdings will establish three new wholly-owned entities—Temasek Global Investments, Temasek Singapore, and Temasek Partnership Solutions—to manage distinct portfolio segments. The Government retains accountability for long-term returns on the overall portfolio, and Temasek will continue publishing net returns after deducting all investment fees and expenses. Further details on the new entities are available via Temasek's press releases and statements.

results.title: Managing Granting of Permits for Road Works to Minimise Road Excavations and Disruption to Motorists
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority (LTA) coordinates road work scheduling to stagger multiple demands and optimise efficiency. Permits limit work duration to three weeks with daily fees to incentivise expeditious completion, while prohibiting work during peak traffic hours. Agencies must obtain approval before commencing, provide advance stakeholder notifications, and promptly reinstate affected road sections daily for peak hours. Upon completion, permanent reinstatement must adhere to LTA's Code of Practice for Works on Public Streets under supervision of qualified personnel. LTA conducts regular compliance checks to ensure standards are met, aiming to minimise excavations, reduce disruption duration, and ensure timely utility supply, though emergency repairs may occasionally affect planned schedules.

results.title: Measures beyond Imprisonment and Licence Suspension to Deter Drink Driving
results.summary: The Minister for Home Affairs stated that first-time drink driving offenders face fines of $2,000–$10,000, up to 12 months' imprisonment, and a minimum two-year driving disqualification, while repeat offenders face fines of $5,000–$20,000, up to two years' imprisonment, and at least five years' disqualification. Authorities are also reviewing maximum allowable breath and blood alcohol concentration limits to enhance deterrence. Details regarding the new limits will be announced when ready. The core message remains that drivers who consume alcohol must not operate a vehicle.

results.title: Measures to Mitigate High Electricity Prices Arising from Singapore's Ongoing Decarbonisation Efforts
results.summary: The impact of decarbonisation on electricity prices remains uncertain, though tariffs are expected to rise by approximately 1% for every $5/tCO2e increase in the carbon tax. The Government will safeguard energy security and affordability while introducing low-carbon sources. Lower- to middle-income HDB households will continue receiving utility bill support via U-Save rebates. Businesses will also receive assistance to improve energy efficiency, which is expected to lower their energy costs. Specific timelines for system-wide savings or environmental benefits were not stated.

results.title: Measures to Moderate Rental Volatility for Privately-held HDB Shops
results.summary: The Minister for National Development referred Members to a combined reply issued on 24 September 2025 regarding HDB shop rents, which addressed similar questions from Ms Mariam Jaafar, Mr Chua Kheng Wee Louis, Mr Kwek Hian Chuan Henry, and Mr Ang Wei Neng. The response did not introduce new measures to moderate rental volatility for privately-held HDB shops or establish rent control. Consequently, no specific actions, timelines, or eligibility rules for moderating rental volatility were stated in this session.

results.title: Measures to Preserve Alternative Cultural Spaces Beyond Mainstream Venues
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth directed that the response regarding steps to preserve alternative cultural spaces, such as independent cinemas and venue-based hubs, be provided in the reply to Question No 31 for the oral answer on the Order Paper for the Sitting on 25 September 2025. No specific measures, timelines, or operational details were stated in this transcript. The substantive policy information is contained in the referenced parliamentary record rather than the current debate text.

results.title: Measures to Safeguard Long-term Financial Security of Family Caregivers Who Left Workforce or Reduced Work Hours
results.summary: The Ministry supports caregivers' financial security by promoting flexible work arrangements, reskilling, and eligibility for the Workfare Income Supplement. From 1 April 2025, parents gain access to enhanced Government-Paid Paternity Leave and a new Shared Parental Leave scheme. Caregivers can utilize subsidies, grants, and insurance enhancements for long-term care, including the Home Caregiving Grant and CareShield Life. Young children receive support via the Baby Bonus Cash Gift and preschool subsidies. Seniors benefit from the enhanced Silver Support Scheme, Matched Retirement Savings Scheme (effective January 2025), and the Majulah Package for those born in 1973 or earlier. The Government, employers, and community share responsibility for sustainable caregiving balance, with ongoing reviews planned for evolving support needs.

results.title: Measures to Stop Active Mobility Users on Park Connector Networks from Blasting Music When Close To Housing Estates
results.summary: National Parks Board (NParks) will step up enforcement operations and public education efforts to address active mobility users blasting loud music near housing estates along Park Connector Networks. Existing signage advises users to keep noise levels down, while NParks collaborates with partners like the People's Association and Residents' Networks to raise awareness on PCN etiquette. The Minister stated that visitors should be considerate and avoid creating noise that disturbs the wider community, particularly during quiet hours. No specific new legislative measures or revised timelines were announced in this response.

results.title: Metrics Used to Establish Affordability of New HDB BTO Flats
results.summary: The Minister stated the Government uses a holistic approach to assess housing affordability, tracking indicators such as the house price-to-income ratio and mortgage servicing ratio across various household income levels, flat types, and locations. To maintain affordability, significant market discounts are applied to new Build-To-Order (BTO) flats, complemented by Enhanced Central Provident Fund (CPF) Housing Grants of up to $120,000 for eligible first-timer families. In 2024, approximately 90% of first-timer families servicing BTO loans achieved mortgage servicing ratios of 25% or less using CPF, surpassing international benchmarks of 30% to 35%. Specific quantitative thresholds for income percentiles or loan tenure were not explicitly detailed in the response.

results.title: Minimum Occupation Period Appeals for Early Sale of HDB Flats and Policy Review Considerations
results.summary: From 2022 to 2024, HDB approved approximately 1,700 early sale requests, including about 50 for families outgrowing their homes. The Minister stated the Minimum Occupation Period (MOP) remains essential to safeguard housing for genuine needs and maintain affordability, noting most households reside in flats for 10+ years. Under the new classification framework, a 10-year MOP for Prime and Plus flats balances owner-occupation intent with flexibility for life changes like growing families. The Ministry will not review its MOP waiver position based on the new framework, as the current 10-year duration already provides adequate balance.

results.title: Mix of Denominations in CDC Vouchers
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth stated that Community Development Council (CDC) vouchers maintain fixed denominations of $2, $5, and $10 to ensure spending flexibility and reduce payment errors for less digital-savvy residents, particularly seniors. While up to 15 vouchers can be stacked digitally for larger transactions, the system does not currently offer a review of the no-change policy or additional denomination flexibility for specific household segments. This design supports usage across hawkers, heartland merchants, and supermarkets, with households spending approximately $2.3 billion between the scheme's December 2021 launch and July 2025. The Minister noted that larger denominations like $10 or $20 for SG60 Vouchers are specifically suited for higher-value purchases at supermarkets.

results.title: Monitored Claims against Investment-linked Policies for Every Year Since 2015
results.summary: The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) monitors investment-linked policy (ILP) disputes via the Financial Industry Disputes Resolution Centre (FIDReC), which handled an average of 83 annual cases from 2017 to 2024, rising to 211 in 2024. Median claim amounts decreased from $11,300 in 2017 to $7,200 in 2024. MAS enforces suitability requirements, disclosure of fees, and special arrangements for vulnerable clients, with stricter rules effective 29 December 2025 mandating a trusted individual's presence during sales. MAS proposes classifying ILPs as complex products requiring red warning bands and mandatory financial advice for vulnerable buyers, aiming to finalise these measures in early 2026 following a July public consultation.

results.title: Multilingual Options for ScamShield Helpline and Outreach Materials
results.summary: The Police will enhance the ScamShield Interactive Voice Response System to support navigation in all four official languages starting in 2026, following feedback from seniors. While operator assistance remains available in preferred languages when possible, availability is not guaranteed during peak hours or at night. Multilingual anti-scam resource guides in all four official languages have been disseminated to households since July and are accessible via community partners, Neighbourhood Police Centres, and the ScamShield website. The Minister indicated that the team is working to deliver the multi-language IVR feature as soon as possible within 2026 in concert with the National Crime Prevention Council. Seniors can request operators to slow down their speech if needed, and the ScamShield suite of options including the website and app serves as key resources for scam clarifications.

results.title: Number of Feedback and Complaints on Bird Nuisance and Most Successful Method Used in Past Two Years
results.summary: The Minister for National Development directed that the written reply to Question No. 44 on the Order Paper dated 23 September 2025 be provided, covering the number of feedback and complaints on bird nuisance received by the Ministry and its agencies over the past two years. The response also details the most successful method used to manage bird populations and confirms whether new methods are being deployed to address these issues. Specific statistics, operational constraints, and programme names were not included in the transcript.

results.title: Number of Singaporeans who Have Not Yet Used SkillsFuture Credits
results.summary: As of 31 December 2024, more than one-in-three eligible Singaporeans have utilised their SkillsFuture Credits, though this metric does not fully capture total training participation since many individuals attend sponsored or self-funded courses without using credits. The Minister clarified that base-tier credits cover a wide range of skills-based courses, including technology areas like artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, while additional-tier credits apply to over 7,000 employability-focused courses. To drive further utilisation, SkillsFuture Singapore will enhance the MySkillsFuture portal with personalised recommendations and collaborate with agencies such as the People's Association and Community Development Councils to raise awareness. These outreach efforts specifically target seniors and individuals needing assistance navigating the portal or signing up for relevant training courses.

results.title: Out-of-pocket Healthcare Costs for Chronic Conditions in CDMP by CHAS Tier, Housing Type and Income Quintiles over Past Five Years
results.summary: The Minister for Health stated that comprehensive data on median out-of-pocket payments for the top 10 Chronic Disease Management Programme conditions, disaggregated by CHAS tier, housing type, and income quintiles, is unavailable. However, Tables 1 and 2 provide the median post-subsidy amounts for these conditions from 2019 to 2023. Consequently, the specific breakdown requested by the Member could not be fully addressed as the underlying granular data does not exist.

results.title: Percentage of Jointly-created Intellectual Properties Generating Little or No Licensing Revenue for Singapore
results.summary: Under the RIE 2025 plan, approximately 80% of Corporate Laboratory projects supported by the Industry Alignment Fund-Industry Collaboration Projects scheme are expected to generate licensing revenue from jointly-created intellectual property. Beyond direct revenue, these collaborations aim to foster economic growth by enabling partner companies to develop new products and training a skilled research workforce. Specific initiatives, such as the $110 million Wilmar-NUS Corporate Laboratory, have already trained over 70 researchers and developed IP with potential licensing revenue of US$25 million. Other examples include the Agency for Science, Technology and Research's Institute of Microelectronics partnership, which has created more than 120 innovation jobs since 2011 and attracted global platforms like Applied Materials to host their advanced chip packaging technologies in Singapore.

results.title: Plans to Enhance Ecological Connectivity between Mandai Mangrove and Mudflats to Central Catchment Nature Reserve
results.summary: The National Parks Board is establishing a new Kranji Nature Corridor to strengthen ecological connectivity between the Central Catchment Nature Reserve and Mandai Mangrove and Mudflat Nature Park. This initiative includes over 110 hectares of new parks, approximately 8 km of recreational routes, and more than 11 km of nature ways featuring plantings to replicate forest structures and facilitate animal movement. NParks is also studying potential parks at Sungei Pang Sua and Kranji Reservoir, while agencies will assess the need for Environmental Impact Assessments for buffer forests north of the Central Catchment Nature Reserve if future development plans arise.

results.title: Post-study and Employment Trends for Graduating Students of ITEs, Polytechnics, Autonomous Universities and Private Universities
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the requested data on employment trends, including full-time versus fixed-term employment rates and median contract durations for ITE, polytechnic, autonomous university, and private university graduates, has been provided via a written answer dated 22 September 2025. Members are directed to refer to Question No 58 in the 22 September Order Paper for the specific statistics covering the past five years. No further details were discussed during the parliamentary session.

results.title: Pre-emptive Measures to Prevent Sinkhole Formation at PUB Job Sites
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated that the question regarding pre-emptive measures to prevent sinkhole formation at PUB job sites was previously addressed via written answer on 22 September 2025. Interested parties are directed to refer to the responses to Questions 104, 105, and 106 in the 22 September 2025 Order Paper for the specific details. No new measures or clarifications were provided during this session.

results.title: Precincts with High-density Condominiums and Apartments Experiencing Poor Mobile Connectivity and Enhancement Plans
results.summary: The Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA) mandates mobile network operators to achieve at least 99% outdoor and 85% indoor 4G coverage. Operators failing these standards or receiving specific feedback must resolve coverage gaps within six months by deploying additional equipment. This year, operators resolved approximately half of reported complaints through such deployments while actively seeking necessary approvals from agencies and building owners to address remaining issues. The Ministry tracks these complaints and works with operators to ensure good mobile coverage for Singaporeans.

results.title: Preservation of Local Cinematic Diversity and Redevelopment of Cinema Spaces
results.summary: The Minister clarified that cinemas operate as commercial entities where the Government does not prescribe content models or intervene in operator decisions regarding space redevelopment, which remains the responsibility of landowners. While direct intervention in commercial closures is not possible, the Government supports the cultural ecosystem through grants for local filmmakers, arts organisations, and key cultural programmes, noting that local productions like *Monster Island* and *Ajoomma* have achieved success via online streaming platforms. For displaced employees, Workforce Singapore provides career services, while eligible Singapore Citizens earning below S$5,000 monthly who face involuntary unemployment due to business cessation can apply for the SkillsFuture Jobseeker Support Scheme, offering up to S$6,000 in temporary financial assistance over six months.

results.title: Profile of Staff Retrenched from Mediacorp in September 2025
results.summary: On 1 September 2025, Mediacorp announced the retrenchment of 93 staff after informing the Ministry of Manpower. The Ministry cannot disclose the age range of affected employees as these details are confidential. Affected staff were offered opportunities to apply for alternative roles within Mediacorp. The company engaged the Singapore Union of Broadcasting Employees to support them and provided a training grant for skills upgrading. Additionally, Mediacorp is collaborating with the NTUC Employment and Employability Institute to deliver employment facilitation and outplacement services.

results.title: Progress of Jurong Region MRT Line Development
results.summary: As of end August 2025, Jurong Region Line (JRL) Stage 1 is over 70% complete, with phased operations scheduled between 2027 and 2029. The Land Transport Authority (LTA) currently provides bus services connecting Nanyang residents to transport nodes, including the Nanyang Technological University stations, and monitors demand under the Bus Connectivity Enhancement Programme. While a direct bus link from Jurong West Streets 91 and 93 to the upcoming Gek Poh MRT station is requested, LTA plans routes based on expressed needs and balances infrastructure readiness. Further details on JRL progress will be shared in early 2026 after reaching a civil works milestone.

results.title: Projected Demand for Childminding Services over Three-year Pilot Period
results.summary: The Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) launched a three-year Childminding Pilot for Infants in December 2024 to support infants aged two to 18 months. As of the reporting period, the pilot has enrolled approximately 50 infants across childminders' homes and community premises, with around 160 families indicating interest in the next six months, including expectant parents. ECDA attributes current uptake levels to ongoing efforts to enhance public awareness rather than specific barriers like cost or location. The agency will continue to study demand, work with operators to improve matching efficiency, and refine the model based on parent feedback throughout the pilot duration.

results.title: Projected Impact of Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone on Cross-border Investment, Job Creation and Supply Chain Resilience
results.summary: The Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ), established following the January 2025 agreement, leverages Singapore's financial hub status with Johor's land resources to attract cross-border investment. Since signing, the zone has secured RM 56 billion in approved investments in the first half of 2025. Singapore-based firms like Agrocorp, Day One, and ResMed are expanding operations across the border, creating jobs and enhancing supply chain resilience through a hub-and-spoke model. These initiatives anchor operations in Singapore while utilizing Johor facilities, directly benefiting Singaporeans through employment and ecosystem growth. The Deputy Prime Minister confirmed the project's progress and its sustainable benefits for local workers.

results.title: Proposal for Formation of "Algorithm Committee" to Look into Allocation Mechanisms of E-commerce, Ride-hail, Delivery and Online Platforms
results.summary: The Government maintains existing measures to ensure fairness and transparency in platform allocation mechanisms, including enforcement powers under the Competition Act and Consumer Protection (Fair Trading) Act, alongside the AI Markets toolkit for best practices. For ride-hailing, the market remains open by prohibiting exclusive driver arrangements and ensuring fare transparency, while the Platform Workers Act establishes a representation framework for task allocation issues. Major operators have agreed on principles for fair earnings and safe incentives. While the Minister noted the Government will monitor AI developments and consider forming an "Algorithm Committee" if new risks emerge, no such committee has been established at this time.

results.title: Proposal for Legislative Review to Build Workplace Trust and Better Protect Privacy of Employees on Medical Leave
results.summary: The Ministry for Manpower maintains that existing legislation, including the Employment Act, Personal Data Protection Act, and Workplace Fairness Act, sufficiently safeguards employment standards while encouraging progressive workplace practices. Under this framework, employers are legally required to excuse employees certified unfit for work by a medical practitioner, yet they must not collect personal data to discourage sick leave usage. While employers retain the right to investigate suspected misconduct, employees facing concerns about data collection or employment policies are directed to utilize internal grievance channels, approach their union, or seek assistance from the Tripartite Alliance for Fair & Progressive Employment Practices. No specific legislative review timeline or explicit prohibition on tracking practices was announced.

results.title: Rationale for Delaying Climate Reporting Requirements for SMEs Listed on SGX
results.summary: The Government extended climate reporting requirements for SMEs listed on the SGX by five years, rather than the one-to-two-year extension proposed by the Singapore Business Federation. This decision follows SGX Regulation and ACRA reviews indicating that smaller, less-equipped companies require additional time to develop capabilities for International Sustainability Standards Board-based reporting. The extension aims to prevent global economic headwinds from undermining competitiveness while allowing firms to build robust data collection processes. The Government will continue supporting capacity-building for directors and management to ensure companies remain greener and globally competitive during this transition period.

results.title: Rationale for Shortening National Institute of Education's Postgraduate Teacher Training  by Four Months
results.summary: On 3 June 2025, the Ministry of Education announced a reduction of the National Institute of Education's Postgraduate Diploma in Education (PGDE) programme duration from 16 months to 12 months. This change follows the development of effective approaches, including asynchronous online learning and the shifting of certain learning to the Beginning Teacher stage. The adjustment aims to better accommodate the diverse teaching force, which now includes mid-career officers, while maintaining preparation quality. The Ministry regularly reviews these programmes to ensure new teachers possess necessary knowledge and skills.

results.title: Reason for Continued Train Disruptions on North-South and East-West Lines Despite Upgrades
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport directed that the query regarding continued MRT disruptions on the North-South and East-West Lines since 2015 be addressed in Oral Parliamentary Questions Nos 19 to 24 at the 22 September 2025 Sitting. The response covers the reasons for service interruptions, the proportion attributable to upgraded components, and the impact on ongoing upgrade plans. Specific statistics, timelines, or operational constraints were not provided in this transcript excerpt.

results.title: Recent Closures of Local Arts Spaces and Additional Support to Promote Vibrancy in Arts Scene
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth outlined the Ministry's multi-faceted support framework to sustain a vibrant arts scene despite challenges like space closures. Financial assistance includes the National Arts Council's Major Company Scheme for multi-year funding, project grants for audience development, and the Organisation Transformation Grant for heritage businesses. To preserve cultural spaces, the Ministry provides affordable arts housing via the Framework for Arts Spaces and subsidised facilities-for-hire. Key programmes like Singapore Art Week and Singapore Festival of the Arts offer presentation opportunities, with a majority of recent events being self-organised initiatives. Additionally, the ArtsEverywhere@CDC initiative and partnerships with the Urban Redevelopment Authority facilitate co-location at commercial developments, while the Government continues to engage the community and private sector to help organisations cope with changing market conditions.

results.title: Reemployment for Former Jetstar Asia Staff
results.summary: Following Jetstar Asia's June 2025 closure, a joint job matching exercise involving the NTUC, SMWU, CAAS, and Singapore Airlines Group assisted affected staff. As of 9 September 2025, approximately 90% of former flight crew secured new employment or interviews. The Ministry of Manpower does not track specific re-employment rates for such exercises. Remaining workers were advised to apply for temporary financial assistance under the SkillsFuture Jobseeker Support scheme and utilize the Union Training Assistance Programme to offset training costs while seeking new roles.

results.title: Release of Aggregated Amount of Allowances Provided to Emissions-Intensive Trade-Exposed Sectors
results.summary: Aggregated data on carbon tax allowances for Emissions-Intensive Trade-Exposed sectors will be released in 2027. The Minister stated that achieving the 2035 Nationally Determined Contribution target of 45 to 50 million tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent relies on a whole-of-nation effort involving industry, government, and individuals to abate emissions and improve energy efficiency. This progress also depends on continued international commitment to climate pledges, the maturity of decarbonisation technologies, and effective international cooperation. The impact of transitory allowances on the 2035 target was not explicitly quantified in the response.

results.title: Requests for Construction of Sheltered Pedestrian Walkways in Private Estates
results.summary: LTA prioritizes covered linkways connecting to public transport infrastructure, including connections to private estate boundaries. Requests for linkways further from stations to major amenities are assessed case-by-case using consistent criteria for both private and HDB estates, covering pedestrian traffic, technical feasibility, land ownership, maintenance responsibility, and connectivity to existing linkways. The same factors apply to all estate types. LTA generally assesses and responds to most requests within two to eight weeks, while more complex cases require additional time.

results.title: Requests for SSO Officers to Conduct Home Visits for Applicants with Health and Mobility Issues in Past Two Years
results.summary: The Ministry of Social and Family Development arranges home visits for home-bound applicants unable to use the SupportGoWhere portal, with SSOs contacting individuals within three working days to schedule assistance. While call agents provide application support via hotline, the Ministry does not track the total number of home visit requests received in the past two years. Urgent cases receive appropriate arrangements, but specific statistics on request volumes and average wait times remain untracked by the agency.

results.title: Requirements for EV Charger Deployment in HDB Carparks and Utilisation Enforcement Measures
results.summary: LTA deploys EV chargers primarily on the third deck of HDB multi-storey carparks to balance EV and non-EV driver needs, with current average utilisation rates around 10%. The Authority monitors these rates and will scale deployment in high-demand areas where electrical capacity permits. To deter lot hogging, EV charging operators have implemented idle fees at specific locations, with details available on their respective websites or applications. Pre-deployment engagement with Town Councils and publication of siting rationales were not explicitly confirmed as mandatory requirements in the response.

results.title: Requirements on Bus Stop Kerb Distance to Facilitate Boarding, Alighting and Stroller Accessibility
results.summary: Bus captains are trained to align vehicles as close to and parallel to bus stop kerbs as possible to facilitate safe boarding and alighting for all commuters, including those with strollers. This alignment considers ground factors such as passengers near the kerb at risk from side mirrors. Over the past year, the Land Transport Authority received fewer than five feedback instances regarding stroller users facing difficulties due to wide gaps between buses and kerbs, despite approximately 1.4 billion bus trips. The Authority acknowledges that not all difficulties are reported and is exploring ways to better assist families with young children accessing buses.

results.title: Restoration Plans for Istana Woodneuk as Heritage Site
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth stated there are currently no plans to restore Istana Woodneuk or leverage its heritage value, despite the building's impending transition to state ownership. This response directly addresses the inquiry regarding restoration initiatives but does not provide a timeline, eligibility criteria, or alternative actions for the site. The position remains unchanged pending future policy decisions by the relevant ministry.

results.title: Review and Enhancement Plans for MediSave Usage Limits for Singaporeans with Long-term Medical Expenses
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health, Mr Ong Ye Kung, deferred the question regarding plans to enhance MediSave usage limits for Singaporeans with long-term medical expenses and chronic diseases. He stated that a holistic reply addressing this inquiry, along with similar Parliamentary Questions, will be provided at the next subsequent sitting. No specific timelines, eligibility criteria, or policy measures were announced during this exchange.

results.title: Reviewing Eligibility Criteria for CDC Vouchers to Ensure Adequate Support for Needy Residents
