results.summary: The Ministry will not shift CDC Voucher eligibility from household to individual assessments, as the scheme remains a broad-based measure for daily expenses that complements targeted individual schemes like GST Voucher Cash and MediSave. Household income per person is used as a criterion to account for larger household circumstances, ensuring support is not diluted. For residents requiring additional aid beyond standard schemes, discretionary assistance such as MediFund and ComCare is available. The Government will continue regular reviews of all measures to ensure adequate support for all Singaporeans, including those in larger households, without altering the fundamental household-level design of the CDC Voucher Scheme.

results.title: Role of PUB in Launching of World's Largest Marine Carbon Dioxide Removal Technology
results.summary: PUB, with the National Research Foundation and UCLA, co-funds Equatic's demonstration project for ocean-based carbon dioxide removal at its Tuas facility, commencing operations in 2026. The plant utilizes grid electricity and existing seawater infrastructure to validate scalable technology that could integrate into PUB's desalination plants to support Singapore's net-zero journey. While core technology originates from UCLA, Singaporean institutions like the National University of Singapore contribute via research on safe by-product management. The project aims to demonstrate the potential of this world's largest marine carbon dioxide removal technology.

results.title: Salaries and Retention Rates of Mid-career Professionals in TeSA Programme
results.summary: The Minister stated that since 2016, the TechSkills Accelerator (TeSA) programme has placed over 21,000 locals, including mid-career professionals, into tech roles via initiatives like TIPP and the Career Conversion Programme. Participants acquire skills in cloud computing, software development, and AI before entering entry-level positions such as AI application developers and cybersecurity associates. While over 90% of participants remain in their roles for 24 months post-programme, the government does not typically track participants' last drawn salaries to determine salary acceptance rates.

results.title: Seniors Aged 65 and Above without Singpass Accounts or Have Not Used Their Singpass Accounts in Past 12 Months
results.summary: As of September 2025, approximately 8% of Singapore Citizen/Permanent Resident seniors aged 65 and above lack a Singpass account, down from 11% in 2020, while 9% of account holders have not logged in within the past 12 months. Despite this, seniors remain regular users of e-services for finance, healthcare, and retirement, with over 900,000 accessing RedeemSG and 490,000 using CPF digital services between March and September 2025. The Government maintains a digital-first but not digital-only approach, continuing to support adoption while providing non-digital options via physical touchpoints, hotlines, and ServiceSG centres for those unable to transact digitally.

results.title: Singapore's Foreign Policy Response to Israel's Rejection of Two-state Solution
results.summary: The Minister for Foreign Affairs and Senior Minister of State for Foreign Affairs addressed the impact of Israel's rejection of the two-state solution in ministerial statements delivered on 22 September 2025. These statements reaffirmed Singapore's continued support for a negotiated two-state solution as the sole viable pathway to a peaceful resolution in the Middle East. The specific foreign policy adjustments or operational constraints resulting from this rejection were not detailed in the provided transcript.

results.title: Singapore-India Partnership Roadmap Initiatives to Enhance Singapore's Economic Competitiveness
results.summary: On 4 September 2025, the Singapore-India Comprehensive Strategic Partnership (CSP) Roadmap was launched to deepen collaboration in sustainability, digitalisation, and skills development. Key initiatives include the "Advanced Manufacturing and Semiconductors Ecosystem Partnership," which promotes business-to-business matching, expedites semiconductor material movement via a "Green Lane," and pursues joint R&D. Singapore and India are also co-developing sustainable industrial parks in Rajasthan and Tamil Nadu to attract Singaporean expansion. Additionally, joint training centres for advanced manufacturing, semiconductors, and aviation maintenance will equip India's workforce while providing Singapore companies with skilled manpower. As Singapore is already India's largest investor, the CSP framework aims to enable businesses to seize new opportunities and expand participation in India's economic potential.

results.title: Social Media Platform Compliance with Content Removal Requests During 2025 General Election
results.summary: During the 2025 General Election, the Ministry of Digital Development and Information (MDDI) did not issue content removal requests for inauthentic accounts or behaviour. Instead, MDDI collaborated with Meta, which independently investigated and removed accounts violating community guidelines, including suspected inauthentic ones. The Ministry remains vigilant against coordinated inauthentic behaviour and will act on content breaching laws. No data was provided on the number of requests made, declined, or acted upon by other platforms.

results.title: Strategies to Reduce Contamination of Blue Recycling Bins
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated that strategies to reduce contamination of blue recycling bins were previously addressed in a written answer to Question No 20 on 23 September 2025. The Ministry did not provide new details or confirm plans to encourage residents to pre-sort and clean recyclables before depositing them into bins during this session. Consequently, no updated operational constraints, timelines, or specific measures were announced beyond the reference to the existing written response.

results.title: Students Suspended or Expelled from Educational Institutions for Vaping
results.summary: The Minister for Education directed that the question regarding student suspensions or expulsions for vaping in the past three months, along with assessments of their effectiveness and other long-term institutional measures, was previously addressed via a written answer on 23 September 2025. Members are advised to refer to the response to Question Nos 108 and 109 in the 23 September 2025 Order Paper for the specific data and details. No new statistics, timelines, or policy measures were provided during this parliamentary session.

results.title: Study on Nuclear Energy Usage and Waste Disposal in Singapore and Public Disclosure of Findings
results.summary: The Energy Market Authority (EMA) tender focuses on assessing advanced nuclear power plant technologies and their safety viability for Singapore, explicitly excluding nuclear waste disposal which will be evaluated separately. The Ministry will continue to update the public on nuclear energy developments to enhance understanding, rather than releasing the full study results immediately upon completion. This approach ensures that waste disposal considerations are addressed through a distinct assessment process outside the current technology study scope.

results.title: Sufficient Workforce Readiness Support towards Singapore's Electrification Plans
results.summary: The Government partners Institutes of Higher Learning and industry to build workforce capabilities for electrification in sustainability, data centres, and transportation through pre-employment training and continuing education. Specific initiatives include the Singapore Certified Energy Manager Programme at the National University of Singapore and the National Electric Vehicle Specialist Safety Certification Programme co-developed by the Land Transport Authority and SkillsFuture Singapore. Approximately 900, 600, and 350 electrical engineering graduates are produced annually from autonomous universities, polytechnics, and ITEs respectively. The Government will monitor jobs and skills trends to ensure a sustainable worker pipeline, incorporating findings into guidance like the Skills Framework for Energy and Power.

results.title: Support Strategies and Funding Mechanisms for Non-Commercial Team Sports and National Team Development
results.summary: The High Performance Sport Institute (HPSI) supports non-commercial team sports through grants to over 50 National Sports Associations (NSAs) for facilities, pathway development, and high-performance programmes. NSAs receive priority booking at Sports Hub and ActiveSG facilities, while athletes access the Sport Excellence (spex) framework, including the spexGLOW grant for full-time training up to 12 months for Major Games. For netball, HPSI provides annual facility grants for the National Training Centre at Toa Payoh Integrated Development from 2029, specialist sports science support, and data-driven game analysis. The Government matches dollar-for-dollar contributions to the One Team Singapore Fund to further bolster athlete development.

results.title: Support for Caregivers During and Post-care
results.summary: The Government maintains structured support systems for caregivers of the elderly and young, including pre-caregiving education and post-caregiving reintegration assistance. Public awareness initiatives like the 2023 "We See You Care" campaign and Families for Life Council programmes equip potential caregivers with skills and knowledge. Financial relief is provided through enhanced means-tested subsidies for long-term care, grants such as the Seniors' Mobility and Enabling Fund, and reduced preschool fee caps. Mental health support is accessible via First Stops for Mental Health, mindline 1771, and peer groups like the Caregiver Support Network. Employed caregivers can utilise SkillsFuture courses, Workforce Singapore reskilling initiatives, and the Tripartite Guidelines on Flexible Work Arrangement Requests to balance work and caregiving duties.

results.title: Support for Residents in HDB Estates Without Hawker Centres and Measures to Ensure Meal Affordability for Seniors
results.summary: To ensure affordable meal options in HDB estates, the Housing and Development Board mandates all rental coffeeshops under its Price-Quality Method tender framework since 2018, and renewing tenancies since 2023, to provide budget meals. This requirement now extends to new buyers of privately-owned HDB coffeeshops. The Community Budget Meal initiative encourages operators to accept CDC Vouchers, with participating outlets listed on the BudgetMealGoWhere platform for public access. Additionally, the Government distributed CDC vouchers to households and SG60 vouchers to adult Singaporeans this year, offering a higher quantum to seniors aged 60 and above to assist with daily expenses. Mainstream media campaigns complement these digital efforts to reach non-online users.

results.title: Supporting Severe Hoarding Cases with Referrals to Relevant Healthcare Such As Cognitive Behavioural Therapy
results.summary: The Ministry of National Development, Ministry of Social and Family Development, and sector partners established the New Environment Action Team (NEAT) earlier this year to support severe hoarding cases. NEAT members, including the Institute of Mental Health (IMH) and the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC), now work upstream to identify at-risk individuals for timely referrals to mental health assessments and cognitive behavioural therapy. Previously, interventions occurred only after breaches of public health or safety regulations. Under NEAT, agencies like the Housing Development Board and grassroots leaders refer residents to Community Intervention Teams for holistic support, while community groups assist with decluttering and befriending affected families.

results.title: Teacher Training and National Standards for Handling School Bullying Cases
results.summary: Questions regarding the proportion of bullying perpetrators completing rehabilitation programmes, repeat case statistics, and national standards for disciplinary interventions were raised by Ms Mariam Jaafar. Mr Foo Cexiang sought data on bullying cases identified by teachers over the past three years, current teacher training specifics, and whether the Comprehensive Action Review against Bullying is studying enhanced teacher training. Ms Eileen Chong Pei Shan inquired about upstream preventive measures to normalise help-seeking, strengthen mental health literacy, and teach stress management skills. Mr Desmond Lee noted that these questions were addressed in the oral reply to Questions 1 to 12 on 23 September 2025, with members directed to refer to that transcript for the full response.

results.title: Total Bill and Out-of-pocket Costs for Premature Births by Singapore Citizens at Restructured Hospitals in Past Five Years
results.summary: The Ministry of Health monitors and publishes the range of total bills and out-of-pocket costs (after subsidies and MediSave) for various hospital deliveries on its website. However, the Ministry does not specifically track or provide bill data exclusively for premature births by Singapore citizens. Consequently, specific figures for this category over the past five years are not available in the official records. Interested parties are directed to the MOH website for general delivery cost data.

results.title: Training Courses and Partners Removed from SSG Listing
results.summary: Companies can partner with five appointed Skills Development Partners, including the Institution of Engineers Singapore and Singapore Business Federation, to identify relevant skills and training courses for job listings. To safeguard learner welfare, SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG) monitors programme quality via post-course TRAQOM surveys and mandatory audits; providers failing these checks lose funding. Since January 2025, 42 training providers have been terminated for failing audit checks or poor performance. None of these terminated providers have rejoined the SkillsFuture directory after the suspension period. SSG will continue strengthening quality control measures to ensure programmes remain relevant to employers and validated by learners.

results.title: Training for School Principals and Vice-principals to Support Well-being and Career Development of Teachers and Staff
results.summary: The Ministry of Education confirms that school principals and vice-principals attend structured leadership programmes, including the two-year Vice-Principal Learning Series and the seven-month full-time Leaders in Education Programme. These initiatives cover human resource matters such as professional development, flexible work arrangements, and staff well-being. Additionally, the Ministry curates executive programmes to support experienced leaders in acquiring coaching skills to foster teacher growth. The Minister stated that supporting teacher well-being and career development remains a key priority for the Ministry.

results.title: Transfer of Patients between Hospitals Operated by Different Healthcare Groups Without Polyclinic Referral to Maintain Subsidies
results.summary: Subsidised patients transferring between public healthcare institutions, including across different clusters like National Healthcare Group and SingHealth, retain their subsidy status without requiring a new polyclinic referral. The Minister of State confirmed this policy applies to all public transfers for continuing care. While the Member expressed concern about potential communication gaps regarding this practice, the Minister stated the rule is currently practised and offered to investigate specific cases where patients may have been incorrectly informed. This clarification ensures patients understand they do not need to re-apply for subsidies when moving hospitals within the public sector.

results.title: Treatment of Prisoners with Serious Health Conditions Who Are Prone to Infections
results.summary: The Singapore Prison Service (SPS) maintains a healthcare delivery system aligned with national standards, providing primary care within the Prison Medical Complex and referring inmates with complex conditions requiring highly specialised treatment to Public Healthcare Institutions (PHIs) and National Specialty Centres (NSCs). Established infection control protocols, including negative pressure cells for diseases like COVID-19 and tuberculosis, mirror those in PHIs, while regular audits by the Ministry of Health ensure compliance. SPS manages chronic illnesses such as dialysis, chemotherapy, and post-operative care internally where feasible, though external placement schemes are utilised when prison facilities cannot meet specific medical needs. The service emphasises that doctors and healthcare staff are attuned to inmate requirements, balancing medical necessity with rehabilitation goals, and will continue to improve settings to ensure meaningful care and reintegration.

results.title: Update on Feasibility Studies for Seletar MRT Line
results.summary: Feasibility studies for the Seletar MRT Line remain ongoing, covering land use, engineering, and environmental impact assessments, with no definitive timeline provided despite the project's initial 2019 announcement. The Ministry of Transport expects to complete the line in phases from the 2040s once studies confirm feasibility, noting that rail projects require careful consideration of future land use, depot sites, and construction disruptions. In the interim, the Land Transport Authority has introduced five new peak hour bus services and enhanced existing routes under the Bus Connectivity Enhancement Programme to address North East region congestion. Further enhancements to express bus services will be shared progressively as suitable options are identified to complement the North East Line.

results.title: Use of MediSave for Specialist Consultations, Prescribed Medications and Diagnostic Tests
results.summary: MediSave currently covers specific costly outpatient treatments like cancer drugs, radiotherapy, chronic disease therapies, and diagnostic scans. Starting next year, the annual withdrawal limit for outpatient scans will rise from $300 to $600. Patients aged 60 and above can use their Flexi-MediSave limit to offset bills for specialist consultations, medications, and tests, with this limit increasing from $300 to $400 on 1 October 2025. The Ministry of Health regularly reviews MediSave usage to balance treatment affordability with preserving funds for future healthcare needs.

results.title: Verifying Individuals' Criminal Records with Government
results.summary: Individuals can verify if their criminal records have been rendered spent via the Police e-service platform. However, licence applications, including Security Officer's Licences, are not evaluated solely on criminal record status. Each application is assessed on its own merits, considering all relevant facts and circumstances at the time of submission. Additional criteria, such as undischarged bankruptcy status or involvement in unlawful activities, are also examined for specific licences. The Minister clarified that a clear record on the e-service does not guarantee licence approval, as other eligibility conditions apply.

results.title: President's Speech
results.summary: The President's Speech outlines a strategic shift toward a "we first" society to address widening wealth inequality and social fragmentation amidst rapid technological change. Key measures include expanding public rental housing eligibility beyond family dynamics, introducing means-tested "curiosity credits" for children's enrichment, and reviewing transitory allowances for emissions-intensive sectors based on decarbonisation delivery. The Government commits to broadening secondary school entry pathways beyond the PSLE, integrating AI into education while ensuring no Singaporean is left behind in the digital transformation, and supporting caregivers through normalised flexible work arrangements and shared parental leave. While meritocracy remains central, the administration acknowledges its limitations regarding inherited wealth and pledges to bolster public institutions to level the playing field for all citizens.
